source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the NASA Super Heavy Logistics Transport open-CAD challenge rover architecture (rocker-bogie chassis with six wheel-hub stations and a central slewing-ring payload interface). No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the main chassis skid for a NASA Super Heavy Logistics Transport rover as a multi-part welded/bolted assembly rather than a single monolithic weldment. The rover transports a 100 metric-ton pressurized logistics module (habitat-scale) across Lunar and Martian terrain, rotating the module >= 180 deg in place for offload alignment, and traversing at least 500 m of 20 deg grade without stopping. Geometric constraints: skid bounding footprint 10 m x 6 m x 0.8 m deep; four corner wheel-attachment bosses each 0.6 m x 0.6 m x 0.4 m with 8x M30 tapped holes in a 300 mm x 300 mm pattern; central rotating payload interface is a 4.0 m diameter slewing-ring mount (bolt circle 3.8 m OD with 72 bolts) through which the 100 t module is attached; four tie-down hardpoints on the perimeter (rated 250 kN each). Material: Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed (E = 113.8 GPa, yield = 880 MPa, ultimate = 950 MPa, density = 4430 kg/m^3). Environments: Lunar ambient -173 C to +127 C; Martian -125 C to +20 C. Load cases: (R1) Static 100 t payload in Lunar gravity (1.62 m/s^2): 162 kN downward uniform on slewing-ring bolt circle. (R2) Static 100 t payload in Mars gravity (3.71 m/s^2) = 371 kN downward. (R3) Driving on 20 deg slope in Mars gravity: 152 kN longitudinal shear + 418 kN vertical reaction at slewing ring. (R4) Rotational inertia: payload rotated +/-180 deg in 60 s yields 20.9 kN*m applied to skid through slewing ring. (R5) Dynamic pothole impact: 800 kN vertical shock at one corner boss (0.5 s half-sine). Pass/fail: (R1-R4) max von Mises <= 587 MPa; (R5) max von Mises <= 760 MPa; all cases max deflection at slewing-ring center <= 25 mm; skid mass <= 18,000 kg; first modal frequency with corner bosses hard-mounted >= 8 Hz; thermal warp between Lunar day (+127 C) and night (-173 C) must produce slewing-ring flatness deviation <= 1.0 mm. Primary verification: structural FEA + modal + coupled thermal-structural. Fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid skid, slewing-ring bolt drawing, and a report listing skid mass in kg, max von Mises per case in MPa, max slewing-ring deflection in mm, first modal frequency in Hz, thermal flatness delta in mm.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The chassis is decomposed into: (A) two longitudinal main side-beam weldments running the full 10 m length at the chassis edges; (B) six transverse cross beams spaced along the length tying the side beams together; (C) six wheel-hub carrier plates (one per wheel station, 3 per side) bolting to the rocker-bogie arm ends; (D) two rocker-bogie rocker arms (one per side) carrying the outer and middle wheel hubs; (E) one transverse differential bar linking the two rockers across the chassis centerline for pitch equalization; (F) one payload deck plate hosting the 4.0 m slewing-ring bolt circle and the 4 perimeter tie-down hardpoints; (G) four toolbox mount brackets on the deck perimeter for stowed lunar/mars tooling; (H) one perimeter solar-array support frame around the deck edge; (I) Ti-6Al-4V M20/M30 fastener set securing wheel-hub plates, deck, and solar-array frame. Interfaces: IF1 side-beams welded to cross-beams; IF2 wheel-hub carriers bolted to rocker arms; IF3 rocker arms pinned to side-beam outer brackets; IF4 differential bar pinned to rocker arm tops; IF5 payload deck bolted to cross-beam top flanges through the slewing-ring bolt circle; IF6 solar-array frame bolted to deck perimeter; IF7 toolbox brackets bolted to deck. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC5 800 kN rock strike at one corner). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the main chassis skid for a NASA Super Heavy Logistics Transport rover as a multi-part welded/bolted assembly. The rover transports a 100 metric-ton pressurized logistics module across Lunar and Martian terrain, rotating the module >= 180 deg in place for offload alignment, and traversing at least 500 m of 20 deg grade without stopping.

Skid bounding footprint 10 m x 6 m x 0.8 m deep; four corner wheel-attachment bosses 0.6 m x 0.6 m x 0.4 m with 8x M30 tapped holes on a 300 x 300 mm pattern; central slewing-ring mount 4.0 m diameter (bolt circle 3.8 m OD, 72 bolts); four perimeter tie-down hardpoints rated 250 kN each. Material: Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed (E = 113.8 GPa, yield = 880 MPa, ultimate = 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Lunar ambient -173 C to +127 C; Martian -125 C to +20 C.

LC1 static 100 t Lunar (1.62 m/s^2) = 162 kN on slewing ring. LC2 Mars (3.71 m/s^2) = 371 kN. LC3 Mars 20 deg slope: 152 kN longitudinal shear + 418 kN vertical at slewing ring. LC4 rotation +/-180 deg in 60 s => 20.9 kN*m. LC5 pothole impact: 800 kN vertical shock at one corner boss (0.5 s half-sine). LC1-LC4 max von Mises <= 587 MPa; LC5 <= 760 MPa. Max slewing-ring deflection <= 25 mm. Skid mass <= 18,000 kg. First modal frequency corner-fixed >= 8 Hz. Lunar day-night thermal warp: slewing-ring flatness deviation <= 1.0 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 800 kN half-sine rock-strike shock at one corner requires explicit time integration.

prompt.title: NASA super heavy logistics rover chassis skid (multi-part rocker-bogie assembly)
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V chassis weldment for a 100 t Lunar/Mars logistics rover: two 10 m side-beams + 6 cross-beams + 6 wheel-hub carrier plates + 2 rocker-bogie arms + 1 differential bar + payload deck with 4.0 m slewing ring + toolbox mounts + solar-array frame + fasteners. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under LC5 governing rock-strike load.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four corner wheel-hub carriers fully fixed for structural; free-free for modal
boundary_conditions.loads: slewing ring bolt circle, perimeter tie-downs, corner impact

parts.name: chassis_main_side_beam
parts.role: longitudinal main beam running the full 10 m length at each chassis edge

parts.name: chassis_cross_beam
parts.role: transverse cross beam tying the side beams together and carrying the payload deck

parts.name: wheel_hub_carrier_plate
parts.role: wheel-hub carrier plate at each of 6 wheel stations (3 per side), mating to rocker-bogie arm ends

parts.name: rocker_bogie_rocker_arm
parts.role: rocker-bogie main arm (one per side) carrying outer and middle wheel-hub carriers

parts.name: rocker_differential_bar
parts.role: transverse differential bar linking the two rocker arms across the chassis centerline for pitch equalization

parts.name: payload_deck_plate
parts.role: payload deck plate hosting the 4.0 m slewing-ring bolt circle and 4 perimeter tie-downs

parts.name: toolbox_mount_bracket
parts.role: toolbox / stowed tooling mount bracket on deck perimeter

parts.name: solar_array_support_frame
parts.role: perimeter solar-array support frame around deck edge

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M20/M30 Ti-6Al-4V fastener family for wheel-hub carriers, deck, and solar-array frame

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 10 m x 6 m x 0.8 m deep (chassis)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +X longitudinal (driving direction), +Y transverse, +Z up; slewing ring centered at origin

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC5 800 kN corner rock strike).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

interfaces.load_path: cross-beam shear reactions into side-beam box
wheel-hub reaction (incl. LC5 800 kN corner strike) into rocker arm
rocker arm pivots about side-beam bracket, transfers vertical reactions into side-beam
differential bar equalizes pitch between the two rocker arms
payload vertical + torque loads from slewing ring into cross-beam top flanges
toolbox static loads into deck perimeter

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. The governing 800 kN rock-strike load crosses the wheel-hub-to-rocker and rocker-to-side-beam joints, so assembly-level DCR is required; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A 10 x 6 m titanium chassis is fabricated as welded side-beams + cross-beams + forged wheel-hub carriers + rocker-bogie arms + bolted payload deck rather than a single monolithic plate; the 800 kN pothole shock and 20.9 kN*m rotation torque cross bolted and welded joints that must be modelled as a multi-body structure.
notes.speculation_caveats: Count of 6 wheel stations, rocker-bogie topology (2 rockers + 1 differential), and toolbox/solar-array perimeter frame are programmatic speculation consistent with NASA Super Heavy Logistics concept; loads, material, and pass/fail thresholds inherit the original verbatim.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
